sources.title: Humanoid Robot Market Worth $15.26 Billion in 2030 — MarketsandMarkets
claims_supported: Global humanoid robot market: $2.92B in 2025 (base year)
Projected $15.26B by 2030
growth_5y: +39% CAGR
growth_1y: +37% (derived from 39.2% CAGR trend)

sources.title: Robotics Startups Hit Record Venture Funding in 2026 — $18.8B in H1 Alone
claims_supported: growth_3m: $18.8B in H1 2026 VC — already exceeding prior full-year record of $14.1B (2021)
Robotics/Physical AI VC more than doubled from 2024 to 2025 ($27.6B across 1,009 deals in 2025)
~16,000 humanoid robots globally in 2025, up from ~1,000–2,000 in 2023

sources.title: Neura Robotics Raises Up to $1.4 Billion at $7 Billion Valuation from NVIDIA, Amazon, Qualcomm
claims_supported: Neura Robotics Series C: up to $1.4B at ~$7B valuation (June 10, 2026)
Europe's largest humanoid investment ever
Backers: Tether, NVIDIA, Amazon, Qualcomm, Bosch, Schaeffler, European Investment Bank
Neura 4NE-1 Mini (€19,999) began shipping April 2026
Timeline milestone 6 (2026-06)

sources.title: Figure Exceeds $1B in Series C Funding at $39B Post-Money Valuation
claims_supported: Figure AI Series C valuation: $39B post-money (September 2025)
15x increase from $2.6B valuation 18 months prior
Backers: Parkway VC, Brookfield Asset Management, NVIDIA, Intel Capital, Salesforce, Qualcomm Ventures, T-Mobile Ventures, LG Technology Ventures
Total funding: ~$1.9B across all rounds
Timeline milestone 4 (2025-09)

sources.title: Digit Maker Agility Robotics to Go Public in $2.5B Deal — SEC Filing Details
claims_supported: Agility Robotics SPAC/IPO at $2.5B valuation, ticker AGLT, expected by end of 2026
First humanoid robotics public-market listing in history
65,000+ hours of real-world paid commercial operation
RaaS pricing: $30/hr current, targeting $2–3/hr at scale
Current operating cost: $10–12/hr
Active paid RaaS contracts: GXO Logistics, Mercado Libre, Schaeffler, Toyota Motor Manufacturing Canada

sources.title: Humanoid Robotics in 2026: The Race from Pilot to Platform
claims_supported: 12 commercial humanoid platforms available for purchase or structured lease in 2026 (up from 3 in 2024)
Foundation model convergence enabling task generalization
China accounts for 43%+ of global robotics VC investment
Why now: VLA models + cost curve + structural labor shortage

sources.title: Deloitte Tech Trends 2026: AI for Robots and Drones
claims_supported: Enterprise adoption drivers: labor shortage, AI capability convergence
PESTAL Technological factor: foundation models as the key unlock
Consumer Trend Canvas: why now — foundation model convergence 2024–2025

sources.title: Goldman Sachs: The Global Market for Robots Could Reach $38 Billion by 2035
claims_supported: Goldman Sachs humanoid robot market forecast: $38B by 2035
Manufacturing costs declining ~40% year-over-year
Market forecasts table entry

sources.title: 1X Announces Pre-Order Launch of NEO Humanoid Robot
claims_supported: 1X NEO preorder launch October 28, 2025
$20,000 purchase price or $499/month subscription
10,000 preorders in first 5 days = $200M committed consumer revenue
NEO specs: 66 lbs, 22-DoF hands, lifts 150 lbs
US deliveries began 2026
Timeline milestone 5 (2025-10)

sources.title: Apptronik Closes Over $935 Million Series A — Apollo Commercial Deployment
claims_supported: Apptronik Series A total: $935M+ (including $520M extension, February 2026)
Valuation: ~$5B
Backers: B Capital, Google, Mercedes-Benz, PEAK6, AT&T Ventures, John Deere, Qatar Investment Authority
Apollo specs: 5'8", 160 lbs, lifts 55 lbs, 22 hrs/day operation

sources.title: China Approves First National Standard System for Humanoid Robots (March 2026)
claims_supported: China's first national standard system for humanoid robots approved March 2026
Covers full industrial chain lifecycle
Political PESTAL factor: China's whole-of-government humanoid strategy

sources.title: USCC Report: Humanoid Robots and US National Security
sources.publisher: US-China Economic and Security Review Commission
claims_supported: USCC has flagged humanoid robotics as a US national security concern
China's national humanoid policy ambitions: 100,000 units deployed by 2027
US policy gap vs. China's whole-of-government approach
Takeaway 2: China vs. US market bifurcation risk

sources.title: Unitree Robotics Shanghai STAR Market IPO
claims_supported: Unitree IPO filing: $610M on Shanghai STAR Market, March 2026
Target valuation: ~$6.2B
5,500+ units of G1 shipped in 2025
Investor stack: HongShan, Matrix Partners China, Meituan (~9.6%), Tencent, Alibaba, Ant Group, Xiaomi, ByteDance, BYD, Geely, China Mobile
~34% market share by global unit volume

sources.title: Morgan Stanley: Humanoid Robot Market Could Reach $5 Trillion by 2050

sources.title: Japan Aging Workforce Robotics Crisis: 570,000 Care Worker Shortage by 2040
claims_supported: Japan: 36.25 million people aged 65+ (29.3% of population)
Care worker shortage: 570,000 by 2040
1 applicant per 4.25 available nursing positions
PESTAL Social factor: demographic necessity as adoption driver
Jobs to Be Done 2: healthcare/elder care staffing gap

sources.title: Fortune Business Insights — Humanoid Robots Market $165B by 2034
claims_supported: Humanoid robot market: $6.24B in 2026 (Fortune Business Insights estimate — higher end of range)
Projected $165B by 2034 at 50.6% CAGR
Market forecasts table entry (upper bound)
